message:
fix: eliminate container cleanup timeout warnings in E2E tests

Problem:
- E2E tests consistently showed "context deadline exceeded" warnings
  during container cleanup
- cleanupContainer used docker stop + docker rm -f approach
- docker stop waits up to 10s (default timeout) for SIGTERM response
- Containers running "sleep infinity" always take full 10s to stop
- This caused slow cleanup and frequent timeout warnings

Solution:
- Replace docker stop + docker rm with single docker rm -f command
- docker rm -f immediately kills (SIGKILL) and removes in one operation
- Reduced timeout from 30s to 10s (cleanup is now much faster)
- Added check to suppress "No such container" errors (expected case)

Performance Impact:
- Old approach: ~10+ seconds per container
- New approach: ~0.16 seconds per container
- 63x faster cleanup in real-world testing

This is appropriate for test cleanup where graceful container shutdown
is not required. Production code should still use graceful shutdown.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
